memberdef.argsstring: (const MeshOrPoints &flt, const MeshOrPoints &ref, const AffineXf3f &fltXf, const AffineXf3f &refXf, const VertBitSet &fltSamples={}, const VertBitSet &refSamples={})
memberdef.name: ICP

detaileddescription.para:
Constructs ICP framework with given sample points on both objects
flt
floating object
ref
reference object
fltXf
transformation from floating object space to global space
refXf
transformation from reference object space to global space
fltSamples
samples on floating object to find projections on the reference object during the algorithm
refSamples
samples on reference object to find projections on the floating object during the algorithm

memberdef.argsstring: (const MeshOrPointsXf &flt, const MeshOrPointsXf &ref, const VertBitSet &fltSamples={}, const VertBitSet &refSamples={})
memberdef.name: ICP

memberdef.argsstring: (const MeshOrPoints &flt, const MeshOrPoints &ref, const AffineXf3f &fltXf, const AffineXf3f &refXf, float samplingVoxelSize)
memberdef.name: ICP

detaileddescription.para:
Constructs ICP framework with automatic points sampling on both objects
flt
floating object
ref
reference object
fltXf
transformation from floating object space to global space
refXf
transformation from reference object space to global space
samplingVoxelSize
approximate distance between samples on each of two objects

briefdescription.para: tune algorithm params before run calculateTransformation()
select pairs with origin samples on floating object
select pairs with origin samples on reference object
select pairs with origin samples on both objects
sets to-world transformations both for floating and reference objects
sets to-world transformation for the floating object

detaileddescription.para: automatically selects initial transformation for the floating object based on covariance matrices of both floating and reference objects; applies the transformation to the floating object and returns it

briefdescription.para: recompute point pairs after manual change of transformations or parameters

detaileddescription.para: computes the number of samples able to form pairs

briefdescription.para: computes the number of active point pairs
computes root-mean-square deviation from points to target planes
returns current pairs formed from samples on floating object and projections on reference object
returns current pairs formed from samples on reference object and projections on floating object

detaileddescription.para:
runs ICP algorithm given input objects, transformations, and parameters;
adjusted transformation of the floating object to match reference object

briefdescription.para: deactivate pairs that does not meet farDistFactor criterion

detaileddescription.para: This class allows you to register two object with similar shape using Iterative Closest Points (ICP) point-to-point or point-to-plane algorithms
